---
title: Tick Sample Reception, DNA Extraction, PCR Tracking, and Storage Management
author: Luís Adriano Anholeto
date: "2026-03-21"
version: 1.0
---

::: callout-important
# Safety Requirements

-   Wear **gloves, lab coat, and safety goggles** when handling tick samples or DNA extracts.
-   Treat all biological material as **potentially infectious**.
-   Perform DNA extraction and PCR setup using **standard molecular biology contamination control procedures**.
-   Separate **pre-PCR and post-PCR areas** whenever possible.
-   Disinfect work surfaces using **70% ethanol or appropriate disinfectant** before and after work.
:::

# Purpose

This protocol describes the **standard procedure for tracking tick samples from reception through DNA extraction, PCR analysis, reporting, and storage** using the laboratory Excel tracking template.

The system ensures:

-   Complete **traceability of samples**
-   Documentation of **laboratory workflow**
-   Standardized **sample identification**
-   Reliable **data management for tick pathogen testing**

# Scope

This protocol applies to all laboratory personnel working with:

-   Tick surveillance samples
-   Tick pathogen screening projects
-   Experimental tick infection studies
-   Molecular detection of tick-borne pathogens

# Roles and Responsibilities

## Principal Investigator

-   Oversees implementation of the tracking system.
-   Ensures compliance with laboratory data management policies.

## Laboratory Personnel

-   Record all steps of sample processing.
-   Maintain accurate data entry in the Excel template.
-   Ensure sample IDs remain consistent throughout the workflow.

## Laboratory Manager

-   Maintains the tracking template.
-   Performs regular data backup.
-   Monitors freezer inventory and sample retention.

# Definitions

**Submission ID**

Unique identifier assigned to each incoming batch of samples.

**Tick ID**

Unique identifier assigned to each individual tick specimen.

**DNA Extract ID**

Identifier assigned to DNA obtained from a specific tick specimen.

**PCR Event**

A molecular assay performed on a DNA extract.

**Parent ID**

Reference identifier linking derived samples to the original specimen.

# Overview of the Tracking System

The Excel template contains the following sheets:

| Sheet       | Purpose                     |
|-------------|-----------------------------|
| submissions | Records sample reception    |
| ticks       | Records tick metadata       |
| extractions | Records DNA extraction      |
| pcr_runs    | Records PCR reactions       |
| reports     | Records interpreted results |
| storage     | Records freezer storage     |

Each sheet is connected through **unique identifiers**.

Example sample lineage:

Submission → Tick → DNA Extract → PCR Run → Result → Storage

# Sample Identification System

Use the following naming structure.

Example:

Submission ID\
`TTL26-0012`

Tick ID\
`TTL26-0012-T01`

DNA Extract\
`TTL26-0012-T01-D1`

PCR Event\
`PCR-2026-03-21-Plate01-A03`

This system ensures **traceability of every sample component**.

# Procedure

## 1 Sample Reception

Record all incoming samples in the **submissions sheet**.

Required fields:

-   submission_id
-   date_received
-   received_by
-   submitter_name
-   project_or_study
-   sample_count
-   package_condition
-   cold_chain_status
-   acceptance_status

Acceptance status options:

-   accepted
-   rejected
-   pending

If rejected, document the reason.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2 Tick Registration

Each tick must be entered in the **ticks sheet**.

Required fields:

-   tick_id
-   submission_id
-   specimen_label
-   species
-   life_stage
-   sex
-   host
-   collection_date
-   collection_location

Update **sample_status** as the specimen moves through the workflow.

Typical workflow status:

-   logged
-   identified
-   queued for extraction
-   extracted
-   completed

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3 DNA Extraction

Record all extractions in the **extractions sheet**.

Required fields:

-   dna_extract_id
-   tick_id
-   extraction_date
-   operator
-   extraction_method_or_kit
-   elution_volume_uL
-   dna_concentration_ng_uL
-   qc_status

QC status options:

-   pass
-   fail
-   repeat

Document storage location immediately after extraction.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4 PCR Testing

Record PCR reactions in the **pcr_runs sheet**.

Required fields:

-   pcr_event_id
-   dna_extract_id
-   assay_target
-   plate_id
-   well
-   pcr_date
-   operator
-   cq_ct_value
-   control_status
-   interpretation

Interpretation options:

-   positive
-   negative
-   inconclusive
-   repeat

Verify that **controls are valid before interpreting results**.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5 Result Reporting

Record interpreted results in the **reports sheet**.

Required fields:

-   report_id
-   tick_id
-   dna_extract_id
-   target
-   final_result
-   reviewed_by
-   report_date

Ensure a **second reviewer confirms the result** before release.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6 Sample Storage

Track all stored materials in the **storage sheet**.

Items may include:

-   whole tick specimens
-   DNA extracts
-   PCR products
-   slides

Required fields:

-   stored_item_id
-   item_type
-   parent_id
-   temperature
-   freezer
-   rack
-   box
-   position
-   stored_date

Temperature options:

-   room_temp
-   4C
-   -20C
-   -80C

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Data Integrity

To maintain reliable records:

-   Never delete rows containing historical data.
-   Correct errors by adding notes rather than removing entries.
-   Maintain consistent identifiers.
-   Back up the Excel file regularly.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Storage and Retention

Suggested retention guidelines:

| Material          | Storage                   |
|-------------------|---------------------------|
| Whole ticks       | Room temp or 4 °C         |
| DNA extracts      | -20 °C or -80 °C          |
| PCR products      | Separate post-PCR storage |
| Voucher specimens | Long-term archive         |

Follow laboratory policies for **retention and disposal**.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Revision History

| Version | Date       | Author         | Description |
|---------|------------|----------------|-------------|
| 1.0     | 2026-03-21 | L. A. Anholeto | Initial SOP |

<button onclick="window.print()" class="print-button" title="Print Protocol">

<i class="bi bi-printer"></i>

</button>
